Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Upstream precursor-supply gene; manipulation may influence total flavonoid production but with broad pleiotropic effects.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PAL1 transcript slightly increased in both Pro35S:MIR156 and Pro35S:MIM156; not sufficient to explain opposite anthocyanin/flavonol phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: 20-d-old stem tissues above rosette under long days.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known enzyme assignment from established pathway; no new biochemical assay in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Canonical first step of phenylpropanoid pathway; measured as early biosynthetic gene.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: qRT-PCR correlation with anthocyanin spectrophotometry and LC-MS flavonol profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Expression changes were slight and in same direction in both altered SPL backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad changes to multiple phenylpropanoid-derived metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Slight transcript increase in both Pro35S:MIR156 and Pro35S:MIM156.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Target for modifying total flavonoid content and precursor availability for anthocyanins/flavonols.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with three malonyl-CoA molecules to form chalcone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CHS transcript was higher in Pro35S:MIM156 and lower in Pro35S:MIR156, correlating with flavonol-promoting SPL state and reduced MYB12/MYB111 in MIR156 plants.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Established pathway function; no new enzyme assay here.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Early committed flavonoid backbone step; expression monitored with altered SPL activity.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: qRT-PCR; correlated to anthocyanin spectrophotometry and LC-MS flavonol measurements.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Likely regulated by MYB12/MYB111 rather than directly by SPL9.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing CHS affects many downstream flavonoid classes.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Higher transcript in Pro35S:MIM156, lower in Pro35S:MIR156 (qualitative).

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target for changing total flavonoid precursor flow.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes chalcone to flavanone in early flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CHI transcript higher in Pro35S:MIM156 and lower in Pro35S:MIR156, paralleling CHS and likely reflecting MYB12/MYB111 regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Established pathway function; no new biochemical assay here.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Early flavonoid step; monitored to assess precursor pathway changes.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: qRT-PCR; correlated with stem metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Likely indirectly affected by SPL via MYB12/MYB111.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Higher transcript in Pro35S:MIM156, lower in Pro35S:MIR156 (qualitative).

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Direct target for increasing kaempferol-type flavonols and shifting flux away from anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroflavonols to flavonols such as kaempferol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Most important FLS gene in Arabidopsis, but its transcript was not greatly changed in Pro35S:MIR156 or Pro35S:MIM156; DEX-induced SPL9 also did not affect FLS1 expression.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression analysis under altered SPL activity and DEX-inducible SPL9 assay.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Stem tissues and 1-week-old seedlings after DEX/CHX treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known family assignment from literature; no new enzyme assay here.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Catalyzes branch from dihydroflavonols to flavonols; expression compared with DFR branch gene.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate-gene expression analysis among FLS family members.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Wild type, Pro35S:MIR156, Pro35S:MIM156 stems; ProSPL9:rSPL9-GR seedlings.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Flavonol changes in SPL backgrounds occur without major FLS1 transcript alteration, implying flux control largely via repression of competing DFR branch.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing FLS1 may reduce anthocyanin accumulation due to substrate competition.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No major transcript change in MIR156/MIM156; no effect after DEX-induced SPL9 activation.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Secondary flavonol branch target, though small contribution in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Small-contribution FLS family member; transcript not greatly changed in altered SPL backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Minor contribution relative to FLS1 per cited literature.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Direct engineering target to elevate anthocyanin pigments and nutraceutical value.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Likely Fe(II) and 2-oxoglutarate as a dioxygenase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ANS transcript increased in Pro35S:MIR156 and decreased in Pro35S:MIM156, consistent with positive anthocyanin accumulation when SPL activity is low.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Established pathway function; no new biochemical assay here.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Late anthocyanin biosynthetic gene co-regulated with DFR, F3'H, UGT75C1.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Repressed as part of SPL9-mediated destabilization of MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shifting more flux to anthocyanins may reduce flavonol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Higher in Pro35S:MIR156 and lower in Pro35S:MIM156 (qualitative).

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Major branch-control target for anthocyanin biofortification or flavonol/anthocyanin ratio engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins in the first dedicated step toward anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Key branch enzyme whose expression is strongly repressed by SPL9; greatly increased (>30-fold) in Pro35S:MIR156 and decreased in Pro35S:MIM156, driving anthocyanin accumulation when SPL activity is low.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR, promoter-GUS reporter, DEX-inducible SPL9 assay with CHX, ChIP-qPCR, spatial expression profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Stem basal-to-apical gradient during flowering transition; 1-week-old seedlings after DEX treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Canonical enzyme assignment; strong metabolite/pathway logic and expression response in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Dedicated anthocyanin branch step; expression anti-correlates with SPL9/SPL15 along stem; DFR promoter activity matches anthocyanin pattern.
Pathway_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Higher DFR expression/activity favorable for anthocyanin enrichment.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate pathway gene prioritized due to strong expression response; promoter reporter and direct-target assays used.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Wild type, Pro35S:MIR156, Pro35S:MIM156, ProDFR:GUS, ProDFR:GUS mu, ProSPL9:rSPL9-GR, ProSPL9:GFP-rSPL9.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day growth; dexamethasone and cycloheximide induction.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: Mutation of three GTAC boxes in DFR promoter reporter
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Type: promoter motif mutation in reporter construct
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: qRT-PCR, GUS reporter staining, anthocyanin spectrophotometry.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: SPL9 associates with promoter but GTAC mutations do not alter reporter pattern, indicating indirect recruitment through PAP1-containing complex rather than direct motif-dependent repression.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher DFR diverts flux from flavonol branch.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: DFR transcripts increased >30-fold in Pro35S:MIR156; reduced in Pro35S:MIM156; DEX+CHX caused ~60% decrease in 6 h.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Target to alter anthocyanin composition and branch flux toward cyanidin-type derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates dihydrokaempferol or related flavonoid intermediates at the 3' position to generate dihydroquercetin-related intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase requiring O2 and NADPH via P450 reductase inferred.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript increased in Pro35S:MIR156 and decreased in Pro35S:MIM156 as part of anthocyanin biosynthetic module repressed by SPLs.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known pathway assignment; no new enzyme assay here.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Late anthocyanin module member co-regulated with DFR, ANS, UGT75C1.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Paper uses F3' H/F39H typography inconsistently because OCR; gene refers to flavonoid 3'-hydroxylase.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can alter flavonoid composition and relative quercetin/cyanidin-related products.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Higher in Pro35S:MIR156 and lower in Pro35S:MIM156 (qualitative).

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target to improve anthocyanin stability and accumulation through glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to anthocyanin-related substrate(s), contributing to anthocyanin decoration/stabilization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript increased in Pro35S:MIR156 and decreased in Pro35S:MIM156, matching anthocyanin accumulation pattern.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Assigned as anthocyanin biosynthetic/modifying gene from prior literature cited by authors.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Late anthocyanin gene co-regulated with DFR, ANS, F3'H under SPL control.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Specific substrate and EC not resolved in paper.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter anthocyanin forms and stability rather than total biosynthetic flux only.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Higher transcript in Pro35S:MIR156 and lower in Pro35S:MIM156 (qualitative).

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential modifier of anthocyanin glycosylation, though not SPL-responsive in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript level remained unchanged in both Pro35S:MIR156 and Pro35S:MIM156 despite large anthocyanin changes.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known anthocyanin pathway assignment from prior literature cited.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Late modification gene included in anthocyanin biosynthetic module, but not SPL-responsive here.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Unlike UGT75C1, did not follow DFR/ANS/F3'H response pattern.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May modify anthocyanin forms without changing total content strongly.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No significant transcript change in either altered SPL background.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target to manipulate anthocyanin/flavonol balance, but study found only minor or no role in anthocyanin regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Stem and seedling tissues examined during vegetative-to-floral transition; no notable anthocyanin effect detected.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: spl3-1 had similar purple pigmentation as wild type; overexpression of SPL3 did not decrease anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis and overexpression phenotyping.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not established; likely minor or negligible in anthocyanin regulation compared with SPL9-type genes.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Inflorescence and hypocotyl-cotyledon/stem-rosette junction pigmentation during flowering transition.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate-gene analysis within miR156-targeted SPL family using mutant and overexpression lines.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: spl3-1 mutant in Ws background; transgenic overexpression lines.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: No direct interaction evidence presented with anthocyanin complex members in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Visual pigmentation and spectrophotometric anthocyanin measurement at 530 nm in stems.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Used as representative of SPL3 subfamily; expression along stem did not notably change.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Known developmental regulator in prior literature; not focus here.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SQUAMOSA promoter binding protein-like (SBP-box) transcription factor.
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No significant change versus wild type reported.
Regulation_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: anthocyanin accumulation in stem and seedling junction tissues
Regulation_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Stem internodes, stem-rosette junction, hypocotyl-cotyledon junction

Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Stem regions during transition to flowering.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression pattern along stem was measured; no major anthocyanin-regulatory role established.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Apical, middle, basal stem sections in flowering plants.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: SPL4 expression did not notably change along the stem, consistent with limited impact on anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No notable spatial expression change reported.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Strong candidate for metabolic engineering to increase anthocyanins or alter flavonol/anthocyanin ratio by modulating SPL9 or miR156 activity.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Acts in stem and seedling junction tissues during developmental progression to flowering; effect linked to declining miR156 and increasing SPL9 with age.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of anthocyanin accumulation and positive shifter toward flavonol accumulation; represses anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression, especially DFR.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression, inducible GR activation, qRT-PCR, DFR promoter-GUS reporter, ChIP-qPCR, yeast two-hybrid, luciferase BiFC/competition assay, coimmunoprecipitation, metabolite profiling.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major developmental switch linking flowering transition to flavonoid branch flux; directly prevents anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression by destabilizing the MYB-bHLH-WD40 activation complex.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Vegetative-to-floral transition; stem base versus apex; seedlings treated with dexamethasone.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_Binding_or_Direct_Target_Evidence: ChIP-qPCR showed SPL9 association with DFR promoter regions I/II; direct protein binding to PAP1 shown by yeast two-hybrid, luciferase complementation, and co-IP. GTAC motif mutation did not alter DFR reporter activity, indicating indirect promoter recruitment through protein complex interaction.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Reduced SPL9 activity / higher miR156 activity for higher anthocyanin accumulation; increased SPL9 activity for higher kaempferol flavonols.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: May participate in indirect feedback affecting MYB12/MYB111 and pathway flux; not fully resolved.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate-gene analysis of miR156 targets; validated using transgenic overexpression and inducible constructs.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Pro35S:MIR156, Pro35S:MIM156, Pro35S:rSPL9, ProSPL9:rSPL9-GR, ProSPL9:GFP-rSPL9, spl9-4 spl15-1, wild type Col-0.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Binds PAP1 and MYB113; competes with TT8 for PAP1 binding; acts on MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex; no interaction detected with TT2 or GL1 in yeast.
Regulation_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day growth; dexamethasone plus cycloheximide induction in seedlings.
Regulation_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: miR156 target site rendered resistant in rSPL9 constructs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Branch selection in flavonoid biosynthesis, specifically anthocyanin biosynthesis versus flavonol accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Anthocyanin spectrophotometry (A530), LC-MS of stem flavonols after β-glucosidase digestion, visual pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Acts as bifunctional TF; here repressive through complex destabilization rather than classical direct activation. Promoter binding to DFR is indirect despite ChIP enrichment.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Known roles in phase change, flowering, trichome initiation, cell size, fertility from prior studies.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering SPL9 likely affects flowering time and multiple developmental traits due to pleiotropy.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR, ANS, F3'H, UGT75C1; indirectly affects other flavonoid genes via MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex.
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: DEX+CHX caused ~60% decrease in DFR transcripts within 6 h; higher SPL activity in Pro35S:MIM156 associated with ~10-fold lower stem anthocyanin and 1.6–2.9-fold higher kaempferol derivatives; reduced SPL activity in Pro35S:MIR156 caused ~4-fold higher anthocyanin and <10% flavonol levels.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression via destabilization of MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex and indirect recruitment to promoter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses DFR and other anthocyanin genes by preventing effective activation complex formation.
Regulation_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Stems, stem-rosette junction, hypocotyl-cotyledon junction, seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: miR156 developmental decline with age; dexamethasone in inducible GR system

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target to reduce anthocyanin and favor flavonol accumulation, though less mechanistically characterized than SPL9.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Observed in stem-rosette junction during flowering transition.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression decreased anthocyanin accumulation, similar to SPL9-type behavior.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supports role of SPL9-subfamily genes in repressing anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Close homolog of SPL9; prior literature suggests activation domain.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target in combination with other SPLs for tuning anthocyanin/flavonol balance.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Stem spatial expression inversely correlated with DFR during flowering transition.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with SPL9 subfamily genes in negative regulation of anthocyanin biosynthesis; double mutant alone did not strongly increase anthocyanin, suggesting redundancy among several SPLs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant analysis and spatial qRT-PCR expression profiling.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes redundantly rather than singly decisive.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Inflorescences and hypocotyl-cotyledon junction; apical/middle/basal stem tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate-gene family analysis using spl9-4 spl15-1 mutants.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: spl9-4 spl15-1 double mutant; wild-type stem segments.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Visual pigmentation; qRT-PCR correlation with DFR.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Spatial expression opposite to DFR supports negative regulatory role, but direct mechanism not tested here.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Known developmental regulator from prior studies.
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No increased anthocyanin in spl9-4 spl15-1 double mutant reported.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Positive anthocyanin regulator; knockdown reduces anthocyanin, so activation could enhance anthocyanin accumulation for nutraceutical traits.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Functions in stem/seedling junction tissues; required for Pro35S:MIR156-induced anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis; PAP1 silencing suppresses high-anthocyanin phenotype of Pro35S:MIR156. SPL9 binds PAP1 and disrupts activation complex.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: MIGS knockdown, expression profiling, yeast two-hybrid, luciferase complementation, co-IP, competition assay in Nicotiana.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential anthocyanin activator within MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Inflorescences, stem-rosette and hypocotyl-cotyledon junctions during flowering transition.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_Binding_or_Direct_Target_Evidence: Protein-protein assays show SPL9 directly binds PAP1; paper cites PAP1 as part of activator complex for anthocyanin genes.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Higher PAP1 activity/expression expected favorable for anthocyanin enrichment.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely positive feedback with TT8/PAP1 expression discussed, but not conclusively demonstrated here.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known anthocyanin regulator tested functionally by MIGS and interaction assays.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: pap1-migs in Col-0; Pro35S:MIR156 background; transient Nicotiana assays.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Forms activator complex with TT8/GL3/EGL3 and TTG1; directly bound by SPL9, which competes with TT8 for PAP1 binding.
Regulation_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day growth; transient Agrobacterium assays in N. benthamiana.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Visual pigmentation and anthocyanin accumulation phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: R2R3 domain mediates binding to SPL9 and TT8.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overactivation may divert flux from flavonols and other branches; potential pigmentation pleiotropy.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR, ANS, F3'H, UGT75C1 and other anthocyanin biosynthetic genes via MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex.
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: pap1-migs strongly reduced anthocyanin at hypocotyl-cotyledon and stem-rosette junctions; sufficient to suppress Pro35S:MIR156 inflorescence pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes in complex with bHLH and TTG1.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: SPL9 negatively acts at protein level; developmental cues

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential anthocyanin-enhancing target, but not functionally dissected here.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Stem tissues under altered SPL activity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript moderately increased in Pro35S:MIR156; no dramatic change in Pro35S:MIM156.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: direct known regulator but not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known regulator assayed by candidate-gene expression analysis.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Part of PAP1/PAP2/MYB113/MYB114 MYB group with bHLH-TTG1 complex.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: No strong expression change in MIM156 stems.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes (known from literature cited).
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin genes with bHLH/TTG1 partners.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target to enhance anthocyanin accumulation; also directly interfered with by SPL9.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Stem tissues under altered SPL activity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript moderately increased in Pro35S:MIR156; SPL9 directly interacts with MYB113 in yeast, implying negative regulation of MYB activator function.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR and yeast two-hybrid interaction with SPL9.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_Binding_or_Direct_Target_Evidence: Direct protein-protein interaction with SPL9 in yeast; MYB113 is known activator of anthocyanin genes.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known anthocyanin regulator examined by candidate expression analysis and interaction assay.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Wild type, Pro35S:MIR156, Pro35S:MIM156 stems; yeast assay.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Belongs to PAP1/PAP2/MYB113/MYB114 subgroup; SPL9 binds MYB113.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Functional analysis in planta beyond interaction not provided in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin module with bHLH/TTG1 partners.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Possible target for proanthocyanidin/anthocyanin regulation, but role here remains uncertain.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Stem tissues under altered SPL activity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TT2 transcript moderately increased in Pro35S:MIR156; discussed as possible contributor but no direct protein interaction with SPL9 detected.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR and yeast two-hybrid negative interaction test with SPL9.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not established for anthocyanin in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_Binding_or_Direct_Target_Evidence: No direct interaction with SPL9 detected in yeast.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Known to form TT2-TT8-TTG1 complex in seeds; no binding to SPL9 observed in yeast here.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Primarily proanthocyanidin regulation; possible anthocyanin contribution unresolved here.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Paper notes increased TT2 transcripts in MIR156 and decreased in MIM156, raising possibility of contribution to anthocyanin regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BANYULS in seeds (from cited literature); anthocyanin targets unresolved here.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Unresolved for anthocyanin pathway in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Key positive regulator; reduced activity lowers anthocyanin, so enhanced TT8 function may increase anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Required in Pro35S:MIR156 background; stem tissues during flowering transition.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive bHLH component of MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex; tt8-1 reduced anthocyanin accumulation in Pro35S:MIR156 inflorescences. SPL9 competes with TT8 for PAP1 binding.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis, qRT-PCR, yeast two-hybrid, luciferase complementation and competition assays.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core activator complex component required for full anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Inflorescences and stems during floral transition; transient Nicotiana assays.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_Binding_or_Direct_Target_Evidence: Direct PAP1-TT8 interaction shown by yeast and luciferase complementation; SPL9 reduces this interaction.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Higher TT8 activity expected favorable for anthocyanin enrichment.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: TT8 is in a positive feedback loop per cited literature.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known anthocyanin regulator tested with mutant and interaction assays.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: tt8-1 mutant; Pro35S:MIR156 background; transient Nicotiana assays.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Binds PAP1; functionally redundant with GL3/EGL3; with TTG1 forms activation complex; antagonized by SPL9.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Single tt8 mutation only partially reduced anthocyanin because of GL3/EGL3 redundancy.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropy with other epidermal flavonoid traits.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes through MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex.
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: tt8-1 reduced anthocyanin accumulation in Pro35S:MIR156 inflorescences qualitatively.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin genes in partnership with PAP1-like MYBs and TTG1.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: SPL9 antagonism at protein complex level

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential positive regulator of anthocyanin, but not functionally resolved here.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Stem tissues under altered SPL activity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression did not significantly change in Pro35S:MIR156 or Pro35S:MIM156 stems.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not established in this paper due to redundancy and lack of functional tests.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: direct known regulator but not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Redundant with TT8/EGL3 in MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Also involved in trichome development from cited background.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes in complex with PAP1-like MYBs and TTG1.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential positive regulator of anthocyanin, but not functionally resolved here.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Stem tissues under altered SPL activity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression did not significantly change in Pro35S:MIR156 or Pro35S:MIM156 stems.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not established in this paper due to redundancy and lack of functional tests.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: direct known regulator but not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Redundant with TT8/GL3 in MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Also involved in trichome development from cited background.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes in complex with PAP1-like MYBs and TTG1.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target to enhance flavonol production, but no SPL-dependent change seen here.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Stem tissues under altered SPL activity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression monitored; no strong change reported.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: direct known regulator but not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate expression analysis of known early flavonoid regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Functions redundantly with MYB12 and MYB111.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CHS, CHI, F3H, FLS1 (known from cited literature).

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Promising target to raise flavonol content; expression associated with SPL/miR156-driven flavonol changes.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Stem tissues under altered SPL activity during floral transition.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of early flavonoid genes; transcript greatly decreased in Pro35S:MIR156 and implicated in reduced CHS/CHI expression and low flavonol levels.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression correlation with pathway genes and metabolites.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important activator of early flavonoid/flavonol biosynthesis in stems.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_Binding_or_Direct_Target_Evidence: No direct binding tested here; function cited from prior work.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: direct known regulator but indirect in this study context
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Higher MYB12 expression/activity expected favorable for kaempferol flavonol enrichment.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible feedback from anthocyanin accumulation discussed; SPL effect likely indirect.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate expression analysis of known flavonol regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Early flavonoid biosynthesis and flavonol production.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: qRT-PCR correlated with LC-MS flavonol measurements.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Paper states MYB12 and MYB111 were greatly decreased in Pro35S:MIR156; Discussion contains one sentence likely typo regarding direction in MIM156/MIR156.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher flux to flavonols may reduce anthocyanin branch output.
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Greatly decreased transcript level in Pro35S:MIR156 stems; no large increase in MIM156 emphasized.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CHS and CHI and other early flavonoid genes.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Likely indirect downstream of SPL/anthocyanin-state feedback

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Negative regulator whose reduction may enhance anthocyanin content; mechanistically parallels SPL9.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Stem tissues under altered SPL activity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Known repressor of anthocyanin complex; transcript level not significantly altered in Pro35S:MIR156 or Pro35S:MIM156 stems.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in this study; mechanistic role inferred from cited literature and discussed in comparison to SPL9.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Repressor, but not shown to mediate SPL effects here.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: direct known regulator but not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Lower MYBL2 activity would likely favor anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate expression analysis of known anthocyanin repressor.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Interferes with MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex by competing for PAP1-bHLH interaction, similar to SPL9 mechanism.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as mechanistic analog to SPL9 in Discussion.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic module via complex destabilization.
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No significant transcript change in altered SPL backgrounds.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses anthocyanin genes indirectly by destabilizing activator complex.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Key positive regulator required for anthocyanin accumulation; enhancing function may improve anthocyanin traits but could affect other epidermal processes.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Required in Pro35S:MIR156 background; stems/inflorescences during flowering transition.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: WD40 component necessary for anthocyanin accumulation; ttg1-1 fully suppressed Pro35S:MIR156 anthocyanin accumulation in inflorescences.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential scaffold of MYB-bHLH-WD40 anthocyanin activation complex.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Higher TTG1 activity expected favorable for anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known regulator tested by mutant analysis in Pro35S:MIR156 background.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Partners with PAP1/PAP2/MYB113 and TT8/GL3/EGL3 in anthocyanin complex; also with GL1-GL3/EGL3 in trichome initiation.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis transcriptional activation complex assembly.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Paper highlights TTG1 as molecular link between anthocyanin biosynthesis and trichome development.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Trichome initiation defects known from cited literature.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes via MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex.
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: ttg1-1 sufficient to suppress anthocyanin accumulation in Pro35S:MIR156 inflorescences.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enables activation of anthocyanin genes by MYB-bHLH complex.

Title: Negative Regulation of Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in Arabidopsis by a miR156-Targeted SPL Transcription Factor
